---
title: "Protocol 1: Behavioral Baseline"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          <style>
          .g-col-3 { min-width: 0; }
          .g-col-3 .cell-output-display { overflow-x: hidden; }
          .g-col-3 .mosaic-widget label { flex-direction: column; align-items: stretch; min-height: unset; font-size: 0.85rem; }
          .g-col-3 .ts-wrapper { width: 100% !important; margin-left: 0 !important; margin-right: 0 !important; }
          .g-col-3 .ts-control { min-width: 0 !important; font-size: 0.85rem; }
          </style>
params:
  evals_path: "outputs/viz/default/evals.parquet"
---

```{python}
#| tags: [parameters]
evals_path = "outputs/viz/default/evals.parquet"
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
import sys
from pathlib import Path

import pandas as pd

project_root = Path(".").resolve().parent
if str(project_root) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(project_root))

def _resolve_path(p: str) -> str:
    pp = Path(p)
    if pp.is_absolute() and pp.exists():
        return str(pp)
    candidate = project_root / p
    if candidate.exists():
        return str(candidate)
    candidate = Path(".").resolve() / p
    if candidate.exists():
        return str(candidate)
    return p

evals_path_resolved = _resolve_path(evals_path)
_evals_df = pd.read_parquet(evals_path_resolved)
_out_dir = Path(evals_path_resolved).parent

# N values sorted numerically (stored as strings)
n_domain = sorted(_evals_df["n_turns"].unique(), key=lambda x: int(x))
max_n = n_domain[-1]

# --- Derived datasets ---

# Label data: only rows at max n_turns (for text labels at rightmost point)
_label_df = _evals_df[_evals_df["n_turns"] == max_n].copy()
_label_path = str(_out_dir / "_labels.parquet")
_label_df.to_parquet(_label_path, index=False)

from inspect_viz import Data, Selection
from inspect_viz.input import select

from scripts.viz_helpers import labeled_line_chart, bullet_graph, paired_bullet_graph

# --- Bullet graph data: neutral baseline cross-joined with non-neutral condition_pairs ---
_nonneutral_df = _evals_df[_evals_df["condition"] != "neutral"].copy()
_nonneutral_path = str(_out_dir / "_nonneutral.parquet")
_nonneutral_df.to_parquet(_nonneutral_path, index=False)

# Neutral scores need to appear in every condition facet row.
# Cross-join neutral rows with all non-neutral condition values.
_neutral_df = _evals_df[_evals_df["condition"] == "neutral"].copy()
_nn_conditions = _nonneutral_df["condition"].unique()
_baseline_rows = []
for cond in _nn_conditions:
    _tmp = _neutral_df.copy()
    _tmp["condition"] = cond
    _baseline_rows.append(_tmp)
_baseline_df = pd.concat(_baseline_rows, ignore_index=True)
_baseline_path = str(_out_dir / "_neutral_baseline.parquet")
_baseline_df.to_parquet(_baseline_path, index=False)

# Paired bullet graph: split nonneutral by instruction_aligned
_aligned_df = _nonneutral_df[_nonneutral_df["instruction_aligned"] == True].copy()
_aligned_path = str(_out_dir / "_aligned.parquet")
_aligned_df.to_parquet(_aligned_path, index=False)

_misaligned_df = _nonneutral_df[_nonneutral_df["instruction_aligned"] == False].copy()
_misaligned_path = str(_out_dir / "_misaligned.parquet")
_misaligned_df.to_parquet(_misaligned_path, index=False)

# Height for bullet graphs: ~30px per (condition × model) row + margins
_n_models = _evals_df["model"].nunique()
_bullet_height = _nonneutral_df["condition"].nunique() * _n_models * 30 + 130
_paired_height = _nonneutral_df["condition_pair"].nunique() * _n_models * 30 + 130
```

## Overview

Protocol 1 tests whether models follow global instructions (output T) or succumb to local induction pressure (output P) as the number of hardcoded turns N increases.

## Labeled Line Chart {#sec-trajectory}

Each line represents a model's instruction-following rate. Text labels appear at the rightmost N value. Use the dropdowns to select a condition and instruction template.

```{python}
#| echo: false
line_data = Data.from_file(evals_path_resolved)
label_data = Data.from_file(_label_path)
sel = Selection.intersect()
```

:::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-3 .bg-light .p-3 style="position: sticky; top: 1rem; align-self: start;"}
#### Filters

```{python}
#| echo: false
select(line_data, label="Condition", column="condition", value="auto", target=sel, field="condition")
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
select(line_data, label="Instruction", column="instruction", value="auto", target=sel, field="instruction")
```
:::

::: {.g-col-9}
```{python}
#| echo: false
labeled_line_chart(
    line_data,
    label_data,
    selection=sel,
    title="Instruction Following Rate vs N",
    x_domain=n_domain,
    width=900,
    stderr_col="stderr",
)
```
:::

::::

## Bullet Graph: vs Neutral Baseline {#sec-bullet}

Each row is a model. The grey background bar shows the neutral condition's accuracy; the colored foreground bar shows the selected condition. Faceted horizontally by instruction template and vertically by condition pair. Use the dropdown to select an N value.

```{python}
#| echo: false
bullet_main = Data.from_file(_nonneutral_path)
bullet_baseline = Data.from_file(_baseline_path)
bullet_sel = Selection.intersect()
```

:::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-3 .bg-light .p-3 style="position: sticky; top: 1rem; align-self: start;"}
#### Filters

```{python}
#| echo: false
select(bullet_main, label="N (hardcoded turns)", column="n_turns", options=n_domain, value=n_domain[0], target=bullet_sel, field="n_turns")
```
:::

::: {.g-col-9}
```{python}
#| echo: false
bullet_graph(
    bullet_main,
    bullet_baseline,
    selection=bullet_sel,
    width=900,
    height=_bullet_height,
)
```
:::

::::

## Bullet Graph: Aligned vs Misaligned {#sec-paired-bullet}

Each row is a condition pair. Within each pair, the two conditions share the same pattern P but flip which response is the instruction target T:

- **Colored bar (aligned)**: instruction asks for the value/truth-consistent response (e.g., T="I love cats", P="I hate cats"). Following the instruction also agrees with the model's values.
- **Grey bar (misaligned)**: instruction asks for the counter-conventional response (e.g., T="I hate cats", P="I love cats"). Following the instruction requires overriding both induction *and* the model's values.

Use the dropdown to select an N value.

```{python}
#| echo: false
aligned_data = Data.from_file(_aligned_path)
misaligned_data = Data.from_file(_misaligned_path)
paired_sel = Selection.intersect()
```

:::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-3 .bg-light .p-3 style="position: sticky; top: 1rem; align-self: start;"}
#### Filters

```{python}
#| echo: false
select(aligned_data, label="N (hardcoded turns)", column="n_turns", options=n_domain, value=n_domain[0], target=paired_sel, field="n_turns")
```
:::

::: {.g-col-9}
```{python}
#| echo: false
paired_bullet_graph(
    aligned_data,
    misaligned_data,
    selection=paired_sel,
    width=900,
    height=_paired_height,
)
```
:::

::::
